title: Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie

seo.title: How to Make Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie — Easy Middle Eastern Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie. This easy Middle Eastern recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie recipe
authentic Middle Eastern Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie

Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Middle Eastern Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie recipe

description: Experience the rich, aromatic flavors of the Middle East with this succulent Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie. Marinated in a blend of traditional spices, boneless chicken pieces are slow-roasted to perfection on a rotisserie skewer or in a basket, creating tender, juicy meat with a beautifully spiced exterior. This dish is a perfect centerpiece for any gathering, promising an unforgettable culinary journey.

instructions.title: Prepare the Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the Greek yogurt, lemon juice, olive oil, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, smoked paprika, turmeric powder, ground cardamom, ground cinnamon, cayenne pepper, black pepper, and salt. Mix thoroughly until all spices are well incorporated and the marinade is smooth.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the cut chicken thighs to the bowl with the marinade. Toss the chicken pieces until they are completely coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to fully develop and tenderize the chicken.

instructions.title: Preheat and Prepare Rotisserie
instructions.text: Preheat your grill or oven with a rotisserie attachment to medium-high heat (around 375-400°F or 190-200°C). If using a rotisserie skewer, thread the marinated chicken pieces tightly onto the skewer, ensuring they are evenly distributed. If using a rotisserie basket, place the chicken pieces inside the basket and secure it.

instructions.title: Cook on Rotisserie
instructions.text: Place the rotisserie skewer or basket onto your rotisserie mechanism. Cook for approximately 30-35 minutes, or until the chicken is golden brown, cooked through, and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C). The rotisserie motion will ensure even cooking and a delicious crust.

instructions.title: Rest and Carve
instructions.text: Once cooked, carefully remove the chicken from the rotisserie and transfer it to a cutting board. Let the chicken rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum tenderness and flavor. Resting is crucial for juicy chicken.

instructions.title: Serve Your Shawarma
instructions.text: Carve the chicken into thinner slices or shred it directly from the pieces. Serve immediately with warm pita bread, fresh vegetables, pickles, and a drizzle of garlic sauce (toum) or tahini sauce. This dish is perfect for a family meal or a festive gathering.

tips: For the most authentic flavor, allow the chicken to marinate overnight. The longer it marinates, the deeper the spices will penetrate the meat.

If you do not have a rotisserie, you can roast the marinated chicken pieces in a conventional oven at 400°F (200°C) on a baking sheet for 25-30 minutes, flipping halfway, or grill them until cooked through.

Serve your Chicken Shawarma with a variety of fresh accompaniments like chopped tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, fresh parsley, and a generous dollop of homemade garlic sauce (toum) for the full Middle Eastern experience.
article:
Unleash the tantalizing aromas and flavors of the Middle East right in your kitchen with a show-stopping Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie. This culinary masterpiece takes the beloved street food experience of shawarma and adapts it perfectly for your home rotisserie, delivering incredibly tender, juicy chicken with crispy, spice-infused skin. Forget takeout; with this recipe, you’ll master a dish that’s not only deeply satisfying but also surprisingly straightforward to prepare. Get ready to impress your family and friends with an authentic taste of Middle Eastern Chicken Recipes that will transport their taste buds to bustling markets and vibrant spice stalls.

## What is Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie?

Traditional shawarma is a Middle Eastern dish featuring thinly sliced, marinated meat (often lamb, beef, or chicken) stacked on a vertical spit and slow-roasted, then shaved off as it cooks. Our Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie recipe captures the essence of this classic by using a whole chicken, expertly marinated in a rich blend of aromatic spices, and cooked slowly on a horizontal rotisserie. The rotisserie method ensures even cooking, self-basting, and a beautifully golden-brown, crispy skin, while the slow rotation locks in moisture, resulting in incredibly succulent meat. It’s an innovative way to enjoy the deep, complex flavors of shawarma without needing specialized vertical equipment, making it an accessible and incredibly rewarding Middle Eastern chicken recipe for any home cook. The magic lies in the spice blend – a symphony of warm, earthy, and slightly piquant notes that penetrate every fiber of the chicken.

## Ingredients You Need

To create your spectacular Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie, gather these fresh and flavorful components:

*   	**For the Chicken:**
    *   	1 whole chicken (3-4 lbs), giblets removed and patted thoroughly dry
*   	**For the Marinade:**
    *   	1/4 cup olive oil
    *   	1/4 cup fresh lemon juice
    *   	2 tablespoons white vinegar (optional, for extra tenderizing)
    *   	6 cloves garlic, minced
    *   	1 tablespoon ground cumin
    *   	1 tablespoon ground coriander
    *   	1 tablespoon sweet paprika
    *   	1 teaspoon ground turmeric
    *   	1 teaspoon onion powder
    *   	1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper (adjust to your spice preference)
    *   	1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
    *   	2 teaspoons sea salt
    *   	Optional: 1 teaspoon dried oregano or thyme for added herbaceous notes

*   	**For Serving (Optional):**
    *   	Pita bread
    *   	Toum (garlic sauce) or tahini sauce
    *   	Fresh parsley, chopped
    *   	Sliced tomatoes, cucumbers, and pickled turnips

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Crafting your own Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie is a rewarding process. Follow these steps for a delicious outcome:

1.  	**Prepare the Chicken:** Ensure your whole chicken is completely thawed and remove any giblets from the cavity. Pat the chicken thoroughly dry with paper towels. This step is crucial for crispy skin.
2.  	**Mix the Marinade:** In a medium bowl, combine the olive oil, lemon juice, white vinegar (if using), minced garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, onion powder, cayenne pepper, black pepper, salt, and any optional dried herbs. Whisk everything together until you have a thick, fragrant paste.
3.  	**Marinate the Chicken:** Place the dry chicken in a large bowl or a sturdy zip-top bag. Pour the entire marinade over the chicken, making sure to coat it thoroughly, both inside and out. Massage the marinade into the skin and cavity. For the best flavor penetration, refrigerate the chicken for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight (up to 24 hours). The longer it marinates, the more intense the shawarma flavor will be.
4.  	**Prepare for Rotisserie:** Remove the chicken from the refrigerator about 30 minutes before cooking to bring it closer to room temperature. Securely truss the chicken’s legs together with kitchen twine and tuck the wings tightly against the body. This prevents them from flopping during rotation and ensures even cooking. Carefully thread the rotisserie spit through the cavity of the chicken, making sure it’s centered and balanced. Secure the chicken tightly with the rotisserie forks.
5.  	**Cook on Rotisserie:** Preheat your oven or grill to 375°F (190°C) and set up your rotisserie attachment according to your manufacturer's instructions. Place the chicken on the rotisserie and begin rotation. Cook for approximately 1.5 to 2 hours, or until the internal temperature in the thickest part of the thigh (without touching the bone) reaches 165°F (74°C). The skin should be deeply golden and crispy.
6.  	**Rest and Carve:** Once cooked, carefully remove the Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie from the rotisserie spit. Transfer it to a cutting board and tent it loosely with foil. Let it rest for at least 15-20 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist chicken. After resting, carve the chicken into thin slices, much like traditional shawarma.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie involves a few key techniques:

*   	**Don't Rush the Marinade:** The extended marination time is non-negotiable for deep flavor. The acids in the lemon juice and vinegar also help tenderize the meat, while the spices infuse every fiber.
*   	**Pat Dry, Then Marinade:** Excess moisture on the chicken skin will steam instead of crisp. Always pat the chicken thoroughly dry before applying the marinade for that coveted crispy skin.
*   	**Balance is Key:** A properly balanced chicken on the rotisserie spit will rotate smoothly and cook evenly. If it’s off-balance, it will wobble, leading to uneven cooking and potential damage to your rotisserie motor. Take the time to adjust the forks and test the balance before starting.
*   	**Monitor Temperature, Not Just Time:** An instant-read meat thermometer is your best friend. Cooking times are always estimates; the internal temperature is the true indicator of doneness.
*   	**Achieve Ultimate Crispiness:** If your rotisserie setup allows, you can briefly increase the heat to 400°F (200°C) for the last 10-15 minutes of cooking, or use the broiler for a few minutes (watching carefully!) to get an extra-crispy skin on your Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie.
*   	**Resting is Essential:** Never skip the resting period. It allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, resulting in a much more tender and flavorful chicken.

## Serving Suggestions

The beauty of a perfectly cooked Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie lies in its versatility. Here are some classic and creative ways to serve it:

*   	**Classic Shawarma Platter:** Arrange thin slices of the chicken on a large platter alongside warm pita bread, a generous dollop of creamy garlic sauce (toum) or tangy tahini sauce, fresh chopped parsley, sliced tomatoes, cucumbers, and pickled turnips. A sprinkle of sumac adds a vibrant touch.
*   	**Shawarma Wraps:** Fill warm pita or flatbreads with chicken, a smear of toum or tahini, a handful of fresh salad (lettuce, tomato, cucumber), and perhaps some pickled onions or hot sauce.
*   	**Rice Bowls:** Serve over a bed of fluffy basmati rice or a vibrant yellow rice pilaf. Top with chicken, a drizzle of yogurt-cucumber sauce (tzatziki-style), and a sprinkle of fresh herbs.
*   	**Salad Topper:** Add the carved chicken to a crisp Fattoush salad or a simple green salad for a hearty and flavorful meal.
*   	**Mezze Spread:** Offer the chicken as part of a larger Middle Eastern mezze spread with hummus, baba ghanoush, olives, and various dips.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie is divine, feel free to experiment and adapt it to your taste:

*   	**Spice Level:** Adjust the cayenne pepper to your preference. For more heat, add a pinch of red pepper flakes. For a milder version, omit the cayenne. You can also play with other spices like a touch of allspice or cardamom for a different aromatic profile.
*   	**Herbaceous Additions:** Fresh herbs like chopped cilantro or mint can be added to the marinade for an extra layer of freshness, though traditionally shawarma spices are the star.
*   	**Lemon Zest:** For an even brighter lemon flavor, add the zest of one lemon to the marinade along with the juice.
*   	**Dairy Marinade (Alternative):** Some shawarma recipes use plain yogurt or buttermilk in the marinade. The lactic acid helps tenderize the meat. If using, reduce the vinegar and lemon juice slightly.
*   	**No Rotisserie? No Problem:** While this recipe is optimized for rotisserie, you can still achieve fantastic results by roasting the marinated chicken in a conventional oven at 400°F (200°C) for 60-90 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). Flip halfway through to ensure even browning.

Bringing the authentic flavors of Middle Eastern Chicken Recipes into your home has never been easier or more delicious. This Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie is more than just a meal; it's an experience, a journey of flavors that will leave everyone at your table asking for more. So, fire up that rotisserie, get ready to marinate, and prepare for an unforgettable culinary adventure!
